title: lilium canadense var. humboldtii
intro: Lilium canadense var. humboldtii, commonly known as the Canadian lily or Humboldt's lily, is a subspecies of the Canadian lily, a perennial plant native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its flavonoid and terpenoid compounds. The health benefits of Lilium canadense var. humboldtii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
